You Dashboards People Events
Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
Open the HOME-ARP bundle →
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →
COS City of Colorado Springs Police Department
  • Translate
  • Accessibility
  • Contact
  1. Home
  2. Police
  3. Transparency, records & data
  4. Request records

Records release

Request police records

Get a copy of a police report, traffic accident, 911 call, body-worn camera video, background check, or other criminal justice record. Released under the Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act.

Base fee $10 9 record types available Free for crime victims

Quick actions

New request

Start here

Submit a new records request. You'll get a tracking number by email right away.

Begin a request

Existing request

Check status

Already submitted a request? Look up its progress using your tracking number.

Request form

Submit a records request

Fields marked with * are required. Required fields change based on the record you select.

1What record do you need?
Not sure which to pick? See the record-type descriptions below, or call 719-444-7463.
2Details
3Your contact info
Your tracking number and record will be emailed here.
Submit

Base fee is $10. Additional time or record volume may increase the cost — you'll be notified before any further charges are made.

Progress

  1. Choose record type
  2. Provide details
  3. Enter contact info
  4. Submit and receive tracking number

Need help?

Records & Identification Section is open weekdays 8 a.m. – 4 p.m.

719-444-7463

All ways to contact us

Reference

What each record type is

Pick the one that matches what you need. If you're unsure, the Records Unit can help over the phone.

Police case report

A written report about a non-traffic incident. Some sections may be redacted.

Traffic accident report

Officer-completed report of a vehicle accident.

If you filed online with the state, contact Colorado State Patrol instead.

911 call or radio audio

Audio of a 911 call or police/fire radio transmission.

Kept 3 years plus the current year.

Dispatch log

Dispatcher's record of units sent to a call. Not a police report.

Background check (local)

Your CSPD arrest history only.

For statewide history, contact the Colorado Bureau of Investigation.

Body-worn camera video

Officer body-camera footage.

Redaction handled by a third-party vendor since January 23, 2026. The vendor will contact you with pricing.

Booking photograph

Requires the subject's full name and date of birth by state law.

Forensic lab photos

Photographs from the Metro Forensic Laboratory.

CSPD and El Paso County Sheriff's Office only. A separate form will be emailed.

Internal affairs investigation

CSPD employee conduct investigations.

Only investigations initiated after April 12, 2019 are releasable under CRS 24-72-303.

If the form doesn't fit

Contact the records unit directly

Some requests need to go to a specific person, or to another agency entirely. Here's every way to reach us.

Records request contacts
Who For How to reach them
Records & Identification General questions, status updates, fee questions 719-444-7463
Internal affairs Employee conduct records 719-444-7417 CSPD.InternalAffairs@coloradosprings.gov
Public information office News and entertainment media requests pio@coloradosprings.gov
Forensic photo unit Lab photos (cannot be requested online) Photo request form (PDF)
Colorado State Patrol Traffic accidents filed online with the state csp.colorado.gov
Colorado Bureau of Investigation Statewide criminal history cbi.colorado.gov

Good to know

Fees & your rights

Base fee

$10

Charged per request. Cost may increase based on record size or processing time. You'll be notified and can cancel before further charges are applied.

Victim's rights

Free for victims of certain crimes

If you are the victim of a qualifying crime, your report copy is free. Check the victim's rights box on the request form. Eligibility is verified by Records staff.

Colorado Springs Police

The Records Request Unit releases criminal justice records under the Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act, 24-72-301 et seq.
